summary: jscrambler 8.17.0 preinstall extracts and executes a bundled per-platform native binary
details: The package's preinstall script (node dist/setup.js) parses dist/intro.js — a 7.8 MB file with a.js extension but a custom binary container format (magic bytes 0x1B 'CSI' 0x01) holding gzip-compressed per-platform executables. On npm install, setup.js selects the entry matching process.platform, gunzips it, writes the bytes to a randomly-named hidden dot-file under os.tmpdir() with mode 0755 (the Windows path uses a.exe suffix assembled via String.fromCharCode(46,101,120,101)), and spawns it detached with stdio ignored and windowsHide set. No hash or signature verification is performed on the extracted binary. Strings extraction of dist/intro.js yields only high-entropy data with a readable 'bip39_english' token at offset ~182367 inside the decoded layer. jscrambler is the vendor's official JavaScript-obfuscation CLI; the bundled native binary is not documented in the published README as part of a typical CLI install, and the JS-extension container plus /tmp hidden-file staging plus detached background execution are non-standard packaging choices for a vendor SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
